title: Boron nitride-ionic liquid composites and their use for energy storage devices
abstract: Embodiments of the present disclosure pertain to compositions that include a boron nitride, an ionic liquid, and a lithium salt. Such compositions may be in the form of a composite and utilized as an electrolyte, a separator, or an electrolyte- separator combination in an energy storage device. Additional embodiments of the present disclosure pertain to methods of making the compositions of the present disclosure by contacting a boron nitride, an ionic liquid, and a lithium salt. In some embodiments, the methods of the present disclosure also include a step of incorporating the compositions of the present disclosure into an energy storage device. In some embodiments, the present disclosure pertains to energy storage devices that contain the compositions of the present disclosure. In some embodiments, the energy storage device is a battery. In some embodiments, the energy storage device is capable of being operated at temperatures above 100Â°C.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A composition comprising: a boron nitride; an ionic liquid; and a lithium salt. 2. The composition of claim 1, wherein the boron nitride comprises a hexagonal boron nitride. 3. The composition of claim 2, wherein the hexagonal boron nitride is selected from the group consisting of particulate hexagonal boron nitride, exfoliated hexagonal boron nitride, and combinations thereof. 4. The composition of claim 1, wherein the ionic liquid is selected from the group consisting of imidazolium-based ionic liquids, piperidinium-based ionic liquids, phosphonium-based ionic liquids, ammonium-based ionic liquids, and combinations thereof. 5. The composition of claim 1, wherein the ionic liquid is 1 -methyl- 1 -prop ylpiperidinium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide. 6. The composition of claim 1, wherein the lithium salt is selected from the group consisting of lithium hexafluorophosphate, lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, lithium bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide, lithium bis(oxalato)borate, and combinations thereof. 7. The composition of claim 1, wherein the lithium salt is lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide. 8. The composition of claim 1, wherein the composition is in the form of a composite. 9. The composition of claim 1, wherein the composition is utilized as an electrolyte, a separator, or an electrolyte- separator combination in an energy storage device. 10. The composition of claim 9, wherein the energy storage device is a battery. 11. An energy storage device comprising a composition, wherein the composition comprises: a boron nitride; an ionic liquid; and a lithium salt. 12. The energy storage device of claim 11, wherein the boron nitride comprises a hexagonal boron nitride. 13. The energy storage device of claim 12, wherein the hexagonal boron nitride is selected from the group consisting of particulate hexagonal boron nitride, exfoliated hexagonal boron nitride, and combinations thereof. 14. The energy storage device of claim 11, wherein the ionic liquid is selected from the group consisting of imidazolium-based ionic liquids, piperidinium-based ionic liquids, phosphonium- based ionic liquids, ammonium-based ionic liquids, and combinations thereof. 15. The energy storage device of claim 11, wherein the lithium salt is selected from the group consisting of lithium hexafluorophosphate, lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, lithium bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide, lithium bis(oxalato)borate, and combinations thereof. 16. The energy storage device of claim 11, wherein the composition is utilized as an electrolyte, a separator, or an electrolyte-separator combination in the energy storage device. 17. The energy storage device of claim 11, wherein the energy storage device is a battery. 18. The energy storage device of claim 11, wherein the energy storage device is capable of being operated at temperatures of more than about 100Â°C. 19. A method of making a composition, said method comprising: contacting a boron nitride, an ionic liquid, and a lithium salt. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the boron nitride comprises a hexagonal boron nitride. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the hexagonal boron nitride is selected from the group consisting of particulate hexagonal boron nitride, exfoliated hexagonal boron nitride, and combinations thereof. 22. The method of claim 19, wherein the ionic liquid is selected from the group consisting of imidazolium-based ionic liquids, piperidinium-based ionic liquids, phosphonium-based ionic liquids, ammonium-based ionic liquids, and combinations thereof. 23. The method of claim 19, wherein the ionic liquid is 1 -methyl- 1 -prop ylpiperidinium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide. 24. The method of claim 19, wherein the lithium salt is selected from the group consisting of lithium hexafluorophosphate, lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, lithium bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide, lithium bis(oxalato)borate, and combinations thereof. 25. The method of claim 19, wherein the lithium salt is lithium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide. 26. The method of claim 19, wherein the contacting comprises mixing the boron nitride, the ionic liquid, and the lithium salt. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the boron nitride is in solid form, and wherein the ionic liquid and the lithium salt are in a solution. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the mixing occurs at a 1:2 boron nitride to solution wt./wt. ratio. 29. The method of claim 26, wherein the mixing occurs at a 1:3 boron nitride to solution wt./wt. ratio. 30. The method of claim 19, wherein the composition is in the form of a composite. 31. The method of claim 19, further comprising a step of incorporating the composition into an energy storage device. 32. The method of claim 31, wherein the composition is utilized as an electrolyte, a separator, or an electrolyte- separator combination in the energy storage device. 33. The method of claim 31, wherein the energy storage device is a battery.
